title: Phenotyping Mice for Prox1-eGFP Transgene Expression
author: Sylvia Masala

steps.title: Dim lights as much as possible...
steps.instruction: Dim lights as much as possible in testing area and prepare items needed ( Figure 1 ). Figure 1 . Set up of flashlight, glasses, andclean mouse cage.

steps.title: Put on filter glasses and remove cap from flashlight
steps.instruction: Put on filter glasses and remove cap from flashlight.

steps.title: Place mouse to be tested in clean cage
steps.instruction: Place mouse to be tested in clean cage.

steps.title: Turn on flashlight and scan from...
steps.instruction: Turn on flashlight and scan from side to side, looking for bright points of light coming from eyes as the flashlight beam hits the eyes of the mouse being tested ( Figure 2 ). Figure 2. Example images of a wild-type mouse andheterozygous carriers of the Prox1 - eGFP transgene. Note: fluorescent signal appears stronger by eye.

steps.title: Only briefly shine the flashlight directly...
steps.instruction: Only briefly shine the flashlight directly at the eyes of mice. Prolonged, direct exposure to the flashlight beam can activate the pupillary light reflex, potentially causing Prox1-eGFP mice to falsely test negative for transgene expression.

steps.title: If there is uncertainty about the...
steps.instruction: If there is uncertainty about the phenotype of a mouse, leave the mouse being tested in a dark place, assess signal from known wild-type and Prox1-eGFP controls, then retest.

steps.title: This method is only suitable for...
steps.instruction: This method is only suitable for distinguishing wild-type mice from carriers of fluorescent alleles. For discrimination of heterozygous versus homozygous expression of modified alleles, PCR-based testing or more quantitative phenotyping approaches are required.
